2604.08299.title: SeLaR: Selective Latent Reasoning in Large Language Models
2604.08299.paper_summary: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) has become a cornerstone of reasoning in large language models, yet its effectiveness is constrained by the limited expressiveness of discrete token sampling. Recent latent reasoning approaches attempt to alleviate this limitation by replacing discrete tokens with soft embeddings (probability-weighted mixtures of token embeddings) or hidden states, but they commonly suffer from two issues: (1) global activation injects perturbations into high-confidence steps, impairing reasoning stability; and (2) soft embeddings quickly collapse toward the highest-probability token, limiting exploration of alternative trajectories. To address these challenges, we propose SeLaR (Selective Latent Reasoning), a lightweight and training-free framework. SeLaR introduces an entropy-gated mechanism that activates soft embeddings only at low-confidence steps, while preserving discrete decoding at high-confidence steps. Additionally, we propose an entropy-aware contrastive regularization that pushes soft embeddings away from the dominant (highest-probability) token's direction, encouraging sustained exploration of multiple latent reasoning paths. Experiments on five reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that SeLaR consistently outperforms standard CoT and state-of-the-art training-free methods.
2604.08299.paper_summary_zh: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) 已成為大型語言模型推理的基石，但其效能受到離散標記取樣有限表達能力的限制。<lb/>最近的潛在推理方法試圖通過用軟嵌入（基於標記嵌入的概率加權混合）或隱藏狀態替換離散標記來緩解這一限制，但它們通常面臨兩個問題：（1）全局激活會將擾動注入高置信度步驟，損害推理穩定性；（2）軟嵌入迅速向最高概率標記崩潰，限制了對替代路徑的探索。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了 SeLaR（選擇性潛在推理），這是一個輕量級且無需訓練的框架。<lb/>SeLaR 引入了一種熵門控機制，僅在低置信度步驟激活軟嵌入，同時在高置信度步驟保留離散解碼。<lb/>此外，我們提出了一種基於熵的對比正則化，將軟嵌入推離主導（最高概率）標記的方向，鼓勵持續探索多條潛在推理路徑。<lb/>在五個推理基準上的實驗表明，SeLaR 一直優於標準 CoT 和最先進的無訓練方法。
2604.08299.author: Renyu Fu et.al.
